Student accommodation in London: Life in the city

There’s nothing quite like living and studying in London. In the capital, there’s plenty to do and see in this vibrant, bustling hub. Whether you’re attending the University of London, King’s College London, Queen Mary University, the London School of Economics, or any other learning institution, you can explore this dynamic metropolis from your doorstep when you stay in one of our many London student accommodation options. 

Our modern London student apartments are dotted throughout the city, close to universities, attractions, tube stations, and all this incredible city has to offer. 

Holloway

Holloway is a favourite among students for its affordability, excellent transport links, and proximity to London Metropolitan University. The area boasts a range of shops, cafes, and entertainment options, as well as the nearby Emirates Stadium, home to Arsenal Football Club. If you’re keen to explore this area of North London, student accommodation like Stapleton House and Piccadilly Court could be your ideal base.

Clerkenwell

Clerkenwell is known for its historic charm, trendy cafes, and creative industries. Its central location and proximity to institutions like City, University of London make it convenient for students looking for accommodation. The area's mix of green spaces, culture and foodie scene makes it popular with students and young professionals. Stay in London student houses, such as Walmsley Studios, Arbour House, or East Central House, to make the most of Clerkenwell. 

Bethnal Green

Bethnal Green is popular with students, thanks to its affordable housing, vibrant nightlife and cultural diversity. Close to Queen Mary University, it offers trendy cafes, parks, and markets like the famous Columbia Road Flower Market, which provides a lively and eclectic environment for student life. Pacific Court, Sherren House, Blithehale Court and Rahere Court are all London student properties located close by. 

South Bank

South Bank is a cultural hub featuring iconic landmarks like the Southbank Centre, Tate Modern, and the London Eye. Its riverside location, vibrant arts scene, and numerous cafes and restaurants make it an attractive spot for students seeking a lively and inspiring environment close to several universities. Moonraker Point and Wellington Lodge are both London student houses situated close by. 

Tottenham

Tottenham is gaining popularity among students due to its affordability, diverse community, and excellent transport links. The area's growing arts scene, numerous parks, and new developments offer a budget-friendly environment for London uni students. North Lodge and Emily Bowes Court are both student properties located close to Tottenham Hale station. 

Book now to secure your student accommodation in London. 

How to book London student accommodation with us

  • Choose your property and room type.

  • Book a viewing or watch a virtual tour.

  • Choose your length of stay.

  • Enter your personal details.

  • Enter your guarantor details.

  • Submit your payment details.

  • Choose the instalment plan of one, three or seven payments.

To book student accommodation in London you must be at least 18 years old and a student in education.

We offer as standard:

7 Day Cooling-Off Period – if you change your mind you can cancel your booking within seven days.

No Place, No Pay – if your offer is withdrawn or you choose a different university, you can cancel your agreement at no cost.

Ready to book? Here’s how to pay for student accommodation in London

You can pay for your London student accommodation in the following ways:

  • Direct Debit

  • Card payment

  • UnionPay for international students

All bills are included with Unite Students, so there are no hidden extra costs. Pay in one, three or seven instalments to suit your finances.

We don’t take deposits but advance rent may be required. This is a small part of the full-year amount, and we put this towards your first rent instalment.

You may need a guarantor for your student accommodation in London. This must be someone over 25 who can make payments if you’re unable to pay rent on time – for example, a parent or legal guardian.

Types of rooms in London

En-suite rooms

The perfect blend of sociable spaces and your own private room.

Studios

Your own personal studio room, with a private bathroom and kitchen.

Accessible rooms

Rooms with more space and bigger bathrooms tailored to your needs.

Non en-suite rooms

Your own bedroom with a shared kitchen and bathroom.

LondonFAQs
View all FAQs

Yes, you can view student accommodation in London before booking with Unite Students. We offer 360 virtual tours, so you can view our properties online from the comfort of your own home. If you want to see the property in person, just book a viewing and we’ll sort the rest. 

London’s population density and desirability as a place to live and study means that finding somewhere to live can be expensive and competitive. If you’re planning to go to uni in London, we advise securing student accommodation as soon as you receive an offer. Properties with Unite Students do sell out, so the earlier you can reserve yours the more likely you will be to get the property and room grade you want.

London is one of the most ethnically diverse cities in the world, and home to people from all over the globe. International students will not only fit in here, they have room to thrive. With people of all faiths, races and backgrounds living side by side, London is a melting pot of cultures. From places of worship to world cuisines, you’re sure to find a slice of home in London. And London’s many universities and further education institutions welcome students from all backgrounds to study here.

Unlike lots of UK cities, London doesn’t have a specific “student area”. The sheer number of universities spread all over London means that students live in different areas either close to their uni or near tube stations and bus routes. A lot of student accommodation in London is purpose-built and offers a blend of independent living with communal facilities to help students make the most of their uni experience.

As England’s capital city and a global hub for business, banking and tourism, London is more expensive to live in than any other UK city. The cost of things like student accommodation, eating out, shopping and nightlife is high. But, on the other hand, public transport is affordable and efficient, and part-time jobs offer better pay than in many other cities. It’s also possible to live on a reasonable budget thanks to the range of choice in London – there are plenty of discount supermarkets, mid-week deals and student discounts available. 

No, you don’t have to stay in halls at London universities. While there are university-owned halls available, you can choose privately-run student housing in London with Unite Students. If you’ve requested accommodation through your university, our local property team will get in touch to complete your booking. There is also group booking available. 

At Unite Students, your safety is paramount. We’ve invested in our London student accommodation to include the highest security features. This includes key card accessible rooms, CCTV at every property, a 24/7 concierge and an onsite service team. You can also use the Unite Students app to report issues, get updates and contact our team.

London may not be the cheapest city for students, but you can get a good price for accommodation depending on where you stay. Living further away from central London can mean more favourable prices, while there are more high-end offerings close to popular amenities within the city. Booking your London student apartment early can help to lock in the best price. 

Some universities will have London student housing available for clearing students. So there’s always a chance that we’ll still have some student flats available in London. 

And, even if there are no rooms up for grabs, you can join our waiting list in case others drop out. If you don’t get your desired uni place, you can always cancel for free with our No Place No Pay service. 

If you’re eager to secure student accommodation in London, you should apply as soon as you’ve accepted an offer to the university. Booking starts from the first week in November for the following September. 

London student housing is in high demand and usually sells out quickly, so it’s best to book as soon as possible. There are 32 Unite Students properties in London. While this may seem like a lot, it’s a big city and our properties are spread out to accommodate different unis. Book now to secure the property you want and lock in the price for this year.
